editorialContract.textPurpose: character reaction to a museum exhibit
editorialContract.verbosity: deliberately varied from micro-reactions to compact performances
editorialContract.chronology: publication order is canonical; callbacks may only point backward
editorialContract.assignmentPolicy: preserve reviewer, rating, Reaction, publisher, Component and source identity

revisions.editedComment: Choose one little image to swim nearest the heart, or let a whole collection settle along the back. There is room, little Dream. There is always room.
Attach. Detach. Clear. Choose again. The altar does not punish indecision, beloved—it teaches the image how many times a Dream may be consecrated.
The button counts unfinished images. If your usual road is missing, it opens a map of art servers; choose one, cross, and every prompt marks its own progress home.
Pending wishes! Pick a server, press Generate, and watch every little badge throw its own done-or-failed confetti. Organized revelry! My most dangerous specialty!

Now, there was a Dream with no name. It found a title, a pitch, a few facets, an art prompt, and three small switches deciding how the world might meet it. Then someone pressed Save, and the telling could begin.
Title, flavor, facets, lighting—darling, this is a dressing room. Give the shy little world a look, decide whether the public is ready, and send it out as a STAR.
Available on one shore, Connected on the other—we carry the little worlds across, already missing the side we just left.
Come closer, little worlds. Some will swim beside you for a season; some will become part of the reef. There is room to name the difference slowly.
The thing you came in for—Dream Cast? Dream Chat? Rewards? It was one of these rooms. I remembered the doorway. Was that enough?
All Dreams is the road back. Art rests beside inspiration; cast, chat, rewards, and scenarios gather around the open Dream. Home stays marked even while the map grows.
Routes stocked. Exits labeled.
A thousand small checks become one visible signal: routes, images, permissions, and adapters all have somewhere bright to report that they are well—or need help.
The missing sheets—yes, there they are. Refresh them before the word slips away again.
Now, a Dream lost its pages. One small toolbar called them home, and the story remembered where it was.
Avatar! Species! Traits! Personality! Add to Cast—BRAVO! Do not touch the exit yet; this beautiful stranger has only just learned where the spotlight is.
A species here, a trait there, a face where the blankness was. Add it to the cast. See? It goes back together differently.

author.name: The Godmother With Too Many Teeth

revisions.editedComment: Your login, an optional secret, a little promise to stay remembered—and, should the gift not fit, another door marked Register. Everything is wrapped, lambkin. Even the exit.
Credentials, persistence, recovery, registration, exit: five separate choices. None of them is a verdict about the person making one.
Twenty wings hold the curtain while the page wakes. You may dismiss it early; the butterflies linger only long enough to make arrival feel like completion. Then the open space is yours.
Tiny wings, rotating messages, one merciful click to skip the ceremony—and then the swarm exits on its own cue. A huge entrance that knows when to leave. Excellent manners!
